title: The 72-Second Signal From Space Nobody Can Explain
hook: In 1977, a telescope in Ohio picked up a signal from deep space so strong the astronomer circled it on the printout and wrote one word: Wow.
body: The signal lasted exactly 72 seconds, matched almost perfectly what scientists expected an alien transmission might look like, and came from the direction of a cluster of stars in Sagittarius. Nearly fifty years later, telescopes have pointed back at that exact patch of sky hundreds of times. It has never repeated. Some researchers now think it may have been a rare natural event, like a hydrogen cloud lighting up briefly, or even a passing comet nobody accounted for at the time. But no explanation fits every detail perfectly, which is exactly why it still gets studied today.
cta: If a signal like that hit our telescopes again tomorrow, would we even agree on what to do next? Follow for more space mysteries no one has fully solved.
description: A quick look at the Wow! Signal of 1977 — one of the most famous unexplained radio detections in the search for extraterrestrial intelligence.
